description: Versioned public REST API for rider and driver mobile apps, covering order management, driver operations, tracking, and location updates with API key authentication.

actors.name: Rider App
actors.description: Customer-facing mobile application consuming order and tracking endpoints.

actors.name: Driver App
actors.description: Driver-facing mobile application consuming driver and order activity endpoints.

actors.name: Platform
actors.description: API gateway that authenticates requests and routes them to the appropriate resource handlers.

fields.name: api_key
fields.label: Organization-scoped API key sent in the Authorization header or as a query parameter.

fields.name: api_version
fields.label: API version prefix (e.g., v1) included in the request path.

fields.name: resource_id
fields.label: Public identifier of the resource being operated on (e.g., order_xyz, driver_abc).

rules.rule_01: All public API requests must include a valid API key; unauthenticated requests receive a 401 response.
rules.rule_02: The API key establishes the organization context; all returned data is scoped to that organization.
rules.rule_03: API endpoints are versioned under a path prefix (e.g., /v1/); the version is part of the URL path.
rules.rule_04: Resource identifiers are public IDs (prefixed strings like order_xxx, driver_xxx), never internal database IDs.
rules.rule_05: Driver authentication uses a separate login endpoint that returns a bearer token for subsequent calls.
rules.rule_06: The API supports create, read, update, and delete operations for all core resources.
rules.rule_07: Special action endpoints (dispatch, start, cancel, complete, update-activity, track) are available on orders and drivers.
rules.rule_08: Webhook endpoint management is also available through the API for programmatic configuration.

given: request includes a valid API key

then.description: Organization context is resolved from the API key and set for the request.

request_authenticated.result: Request proceeds to the resource handler with full organization scope.

given: request does not include a valid API key
authenticated request to POST /v1/orders with pickup and drop-off

then.description: Order is created with the provided payload; tracking number is returned.

order_created_via_api.result: Caller receives order resource with public ID and tracking number.

given: authenticated driver POST to /v1/drivers/{id}/track with coordinates

then.description: Driver location is updated; broadcast event is fired.

driver_location_updated_via_api.result: Location is updated and broadcast to subscribers.

given: request references a resource ID that does not exist or belongs to a different organization
resource_not_found.result: 404 response is returned. The organization isolation ensures no resource leakage.

errors.message: The requested resource was not found.
The requested API version is not supported.

events.name: order.created
events.description: Order is created via the API.

events.name: driver.location_changed
events.description: Driver location is updated via the track endpoint.

related.reason: API keys are scoped to organizations; the API enforces tenant isolation.
The order API drives the full lifecycle from creation to completion.
Driver app uses dedicated auth and track endpoints.
Webhook endpoints can be managed through the API.

goals.description: Versioned public REST API for rider and driver mobile apps, covering order management, driver operations, tracking, and location updates with API key authentication.

success_metrics.measurement: Successful operations divided by total attempts
Errors that auto-recover without manual intervention

constraints.description: Must degrade gracefully when dependencies are unavailable
Sensitive fields must be encrypted at rest and never logged in plaintext

tradeoffs.reason: integration failures can cascade across systems

invariants: sensitive fields are never logged in plaintext
all data access is authenticated and authorized
error messages never expose internal system details
